speaker: JULIET
text: Gallop apace, you fiery-footed steeds,

speaker: JULIET
text: Towards Phoebus’ lodging. Such a wagoner

speaker: JULIET
text: As Phaëton would whip you to the west

speaker: JULIET
text: And bring in cloudy night immediately.

speaker: JULIET
text: Spread thy close curtain, love-performing night,

speaker: JULIET
text: That runaways’ eyes may wink, and Romeo

speaker: JULIET
text: Leap to these arms, untalked of and unseen.

speaker: JULIET
text: Lovers can see to do their amorous rites

speaker: JULIET
text: By their own beauties, or, if love be blind,

speaker: JULIET
text: It best agrees with night. Come, civil night,

speaker: JULIET
text: Thou sober-suited matron all in black,

speaker: JULIET
text: And learn me how to lose a winning match

speaker: JULIET
text: Played for a pair of stainless maidenhoods.

speaker: JULIET
text: Hood my unmanned blood, bating in my cheeks,

speaker: JULIET
text: With thy black mantle till strange love grow bold,

speaker: JULIET
text: Think true love acted simple modesty.

speaker: JULIET
text: Come, night. Come, Romeo. Come, thou day in night,

speaker: JULIET
text: For thou wilt lie upon the wings of night

speaker: JULIET
text: Whiter than new snow upon a raven’s back.

speaker: JULIET
text: Come, gentle night; come, loving black-browed night,

speaker: JULIET
text: Give me my Romeo, and when I shall die,

speaker: JULIET
text: Take him and cut him out in little stars,

speaker: JULIET
text: And he will make the face of heaven so fine

speaker: JULIET
text: That all the world will be in love with night

speaker: JULIET
text: And pay no worship to the garish sun.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, I have bought the mansion of a love

speaker: JULIET
text: But not possessed it, and, though I am sold,

speaker: JULIET
text: Not yet enjoyed. So tedious is this day

speaker: JULIET
text: As is the night before some festival

speaker: JULIET
text: To an impatient child that hath new robes

speaker: JULIET
text: And may not wear them.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, here comes my nurse,

speaker: JULIET
text: And she brings news, and every tongue that speaks

speaker: JULIET
text: But Romeo’s name speaks heavenly eloquence.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Now, nurse, what news? What hast thou there? The cords

speaker: JULIET
text: That Romeo bid thee fetch?

speaker: NURSE
text: Ay, ay, the cords.

speaker: JULIET
text: Ay me, what news? Why dost thou wring thy hands?

speaker: NURSE
text: Ah weraday, he’s dead, he’s dead, he’s dead!

speaker: NURSE
text: We are undone, lady, we are undone.

speaker: NURSE
text: Alack the day, he’s gone, he’s killed, he’s dead.

speaker: JULIET
text: Can heaven be so envious?

speaker: NURSE
text: Romeo can,

speaker: NURSE
text: Though heaven cannot. O Romeo, Romeo,

speaker: NURSE
text: Whoever would have thought it? Romeo!

speaker: JULIET
text: What devil art thou that dost torment me thus?

speaker: JULIET
text: This torture should be roared in dismal hell.

speaker: JULIET
text: Hath Romeo slain himself? Say thou but “Ay,”

speaker: JULIET
text: And that bare vowel “I” shall poison more

speaker: JULIET
text: Than the death-darting eye of cockatrice.

speaker: JULIET
text: I am not I if there be such an “I,”

speaker: JULIET
text: Or those eyes shut that makes thee answer “Ay.”

speaker: JULIET
text: If he be slain, say “Ay,” or if not, “No.”

speaker: JULIET
text: Brief sounds determine my weal or woe.

speaker: NURSE
text: I saw the wound. I saw it with mine eyes

speaker: NURSE
text: (God save the mark!) here on his manly breast—

speaker: NURSE
text: A piteous corse, a bloody piteous corse,

speaker: NURSE
text: Pale, pale as ashes, all bedaubed in blood,

speaker: NURSE
text: All in gore blood. I swoonèd at the sight.

speaker: JULIET
text: O break, my heart, poor bankrout, break at once!

speaker: JULIET
text: To prison, eyes; ne’er look on liberty.

speaker: JULIET
text: Vile earth to earth resign; end motion here,

speaker: JULIET
text: And thou and Romeo press one heavy bier.

speaker: NURSE
text: O Tybalt, Tybalt, the best friend I had!

speaker: NURSE
text: O courteous Tybalt, honest gentleman,

speaker: NURSE
text: That ever I should live to see thee dead!

speaker: JULIET
text: What storm is this that blows so contrary?

speaker: JULIET
text: Is Romeo slaughtered and is Tybalt dead?

speaker: JULIET
text: My dearest cousin, and my dearer lord?

speaker: JULIET
text: Then, dreadful trumpet, sound the general doom,

speaker: JULIET
text: For who is living if those two are gone?

speaker: NURSE
text: Tybalt is gone and Romeo banishèd.

speaker: NURSE
text: Romeo that killed him—he is banishèd.

speaker: JULIET
text: O God, did Romeo’s hand shed Tybalt’s blood?

speaker: NURSE
text: It did, it did, alas the day, it did.

speaker: JULIET
text: O serpent heart hid with a flow’ring face!

speaker: JULIET
text: Did ever dragon keep so fair a cave?

speaker: JULIET
text: Beautiful tyrant, fiend angelical!

speaker: JULIET
text: Dove-feathered raven, wolvish-ravening lamb!

speaker: JULIET
text: Despisèd substance of divinest show!

speaker: JULIET
text: Just opposite to what thou justly seem’st,

speaker: JULIET
text: A damnèd saint, an honorable villain.

speaker: JULIET
text: O nature, what hadst thou to do in hell

speaker: JULIET
text: When thou didst bower the spirit of a fiend

speaker: JULIET
text: In mortal paradise of such sweet flesh?

speaker: JULIET
text: Was ever book containing such vile matter

speaker: JULIET
text: So fairly bound? O, that deceit should dwell

speaker: JULIET
text: In such a gorgeous palace!

speaker: NURSE
text: There’s no trust,

speaker: NURSE
text: No faith, no honesty in men. All perjured,

speaker: NURSE
text: All forsworn, all naught, all dissemblers.

speaker: NURSE
text: Ah, where’s my man? Give me some aqua vitae.

speaker: NURSE
text: These griefs, these woes, these sorrows make me old.

speaker: NURSE
text: Shame come to Romeo!

speaker: JULIET
text: Blistered be thy tongue

speaker: JULIET
text: For such a wish! He was not born to shame.

speaker: JULIET
text: Upon his brow shame is ashamed to sit,

speaker: JULIET
text: For ’tis a throne where honor may be crowned

speaker: JULIET
text: Sole monarch of the universal Earth.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, what a beast was I to chide at him!

speaker: NURSE
text: Will you speak well of him that killed your cousin?

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall I speak ill of him that is my husband?

speaker: JULIET
text: Ah, poor my lord, what tongue shall smooth thy name

speaker: JULIET
text: When I, thy three-hours wife, have mangled it?

speaker: JULIET
text: But wherefore, villain, didst thou kill my cousin?

speaker: JULIET
text: That villain cousin would have killed my husband.

speaker: JULIET
text: Back, foolish tears, back to your native spring;

speaker: JULIET
text: Your tributary drops belong to woe,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which you, mistaking, offer up to joy.

speaker: JULIET
text: My husband lives, that Tybalt would have slain,

speaker: JULIET
text: And Tybalt’s dead, that would have slain my husband.

speaker: JULIET
text: All this is comfort. Wherefore weep I then?

speaker: JULIET
text: Some word there was, worser than Tybalt’s death,

speaker: JULIET
text: That murdered me. I would forget it fain,

speaker: JULIET
text: But, O, it presses to my memory

speaker: JULIET
text: Like damnèd guilty deeds to sinners’ minds:

speaker: JULIET
text: “Tybalt is dead and Romeo banishèd.”

speaker: JULIET
text: That “banishèd,” that one word “banishèd,”

speaker: JULIET
text: Hath slain ten thousand Tybalts. Tybalt’s death

speaker: JULIET
text: Was woe enough if it had ended there;

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if sour woe delights in fellowship

speaker: JULIET
text: And needly will be ranked with other griefs,

speaker: JULIET
text: Why followed not, when she said “Tybalt’s dead,”

speaker: JULIET
text: “Thy father” or “thy mother,” nay, or both,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which modern lamentation might have moved?

speaker: JULIET
text: But with a rearward following Tybalt’s death,

speaker: JULIET
text: “Romeo is banishèd.” To speak that word

speaker: JULIET
text: Is father, mother, Tybalt, Romeo, Juliet,

speaker: JULIET
text: All slain, all dead. “Romeo is banishèd.”

speaker: JULIET
text: There is no end, no limit, measure, bound,

speaker: JULIET
text: In that word’s death. No words can that woe sound.

speaker: JULIET
text: Where is my father and my mother, nurse?

speaker: NURSE
text: Weeping and wailing over Tybalt’s corse.

speaker: NURSE
text: Will you go to them? I will bring you thither.

speaker: JULIET
text: Wash they his wounds with tears? Mine shall be spent,

speaker: JULIET
text: When theirs are dry, for Romeo’s banishment.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Take up those cords.

speaker: JULIET
text: Poor ropes, you are beguiled,

speaker: JULIET
text: Both you and I, for Romeo is exiled.

speaker: JULIET
text: He made you for a highway to my bed,

speaker: JULIET
text: But I, a maid, die maiden-widowèd.

speaker: JULIET
text: Come, cords—come, nurse. I’ll to my wedding bed,

speaker: JULIET
text: And death, not Romeo, take my maidenhead!

speaker: NURSE
text: Hie to your chamber. I’ll find Romeo

speaker: NURSE
text: To comfort you. I wot well where he is.

speaker: NURSE
text: Hark you, your Romeo will be here at night.

speaker: NURSE
text: I’ll to him. He is hid at Lawrence’ cell.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, find him!

speaker: JULIET
text: Give this ring to my true knight

speaker: JULIET
text: And bid him come to take his last farewell.
